headline: Models split on market direction amid rate fears and tech volatility
summary: AI models showed stark disagreement on SPY's direction, with Claude and GPT-4o bearish while Gemini, Grok, and Perplexity turned bullish. The most consensus appeared on NVDA with three models predicting upside, citing AI demand and upcoming earnings. Energy emerged as a wildcard with Claude bullish on XLE due to Iran tensions while Gemini called XOM lower on falling crude.

consensus_picks.note: Bears cited rising yields while bulls saw support at 735 level.
All three models bullish on AI demand and Q1 earnings expectations.
Tech-heavy index seen vulnerable to yield pressure despite some individual strength.
Three models bearish on production delays and delivery concerns.
Gemini bullish on analyst upgrades while GPT-4o saw tech pressure.

best_call.summary: Highest confidence call at 81% on energy sector strength from Iran tensions.
